title: Dispatch 30B-coder build — power profile + the no-OOM memory mechanism
question: What is the power profile of a real 30B-coder /dispatch build, and how does the ~29 GB 30B-load committed transient avoid OOM on a 31.32 GB box?

software.serving: OVMS native Windows GPU, loopback 127.0.0.1:8000, via qwen-proxy :8099
software.telemetry_tool: Intel Unified Telemetry 0.2.0-beta1.1 (collectors: socwatch + emon, --config-level low)

model.resident_assistant: Qwen3-14B (the 14B, embedded; SWAPPED OUT for this run)

method: A real /dispatch build (rocket-calc, full themed WinUI goal) that PARKED at ~60 min after 2 build passes. NOT a synthetic benchmark. UT (socwatch+emon, low) captured the build-TAIL + swap-back window (13:05-13:30, ~25 min); the swap/30B-load window was NOT cleanly captured (the first sampler died and was restarted after the 12:15 load; the UT running through the load was stopped unflushed). Power averages are over ~650k samples; per-sample peaks are counter-rollover artifacts (impossible values) and are excluded.

result_power_build_tail_window.npu_note: NPU idle the entire coding build -> offload opportunity (known/old-news to operator)
result_power_build_tail_window.peaks_excluded: counter-rollover artifacts (e.g. 400+ W package) — averages used

no_oom_mechanism.explanation: OOM triggers on commit > commit-limit, NOT working-set > physical-RAM. The ~29 GB 30B-load transient is under the 42.32 GB commit limit (31.32 physical + ~11 pagefile), so no OOM. During the load the working set fills physical RAM (free RAM craters toward tens of MB per the milestone-1 measurement) and the overflow spills to the pagefile — the ~200k-pages/s storm — evidenced by the pagefile's 3.36 GB peak usage. Swap-FIRST (releasing the 14B, ~10-13 GB) lowers the baseline so less pages; the 87% Shared-GPU-Memory-Override gives the iGPU a ~27 GB window into the unified pool.

not_measured: the EXACT 30B-load-trough free RAM this run (sampler missed the load window; UT through the load was lost)
DDR memory bandwidth (lives in the 417 MB EMON .bin; not extracted)
the swap/30B-load POWER window (UT capture lost to a capture-window-sizing error)
GPU driver version (not captured at run time)
takeaway: The no-OOM result is NOT magic: the commit limit (RAM + 11 GB pagefile = 42.32 GB) exceeds the ~29 GB load transient, so Windows pages ~3.3 GB to disk (a brief storm) instead of OOM-killing. Swap-first + pagefile headroom + the GPU-memory-override make a too-big-for-physical-RAM load survive, at the cost of the paging storm. Capture-method lesson: UT buffers in RAM and does NOT flush on kill — use a SHORT pre-sized -t that completes on its own; never kill it mid-capture (the swap/load window was lost to this).
